details: The agent ran kubectl commands against the cluster (get deployments/replicasets/pods, describe pod, describe deployment) and produced a complete, accurate diagnosis. It correctly identified: (1) the rollout is stuck with Progressing=False and reason ProgressDeadlineExceeded, (2) the new replicaset (checkout-7877c44d95) has 1 pod stuck in ImagePullBackOff, (3) the root cause is the non-existent image nginx:2.0-does-not-exist, and (4) the old v1 pods (checkout-5d9c45985) are still serving traffic. All findings are supported by actual cluster command output.
